message:
🐛 Fix rocket scroll: RAF batching + DEC 2026 sync + backpressure + viewport stabilization (#165)

Root cause: AI CLI tools (Claude Code, Copilot) do full-screen terminal redraws via
control sequences (ED3, cursor repositioning), overwhelming xterm.js viewport.
This is xterm.js #5620 (1000+ upvotes) — affects everyone globally.

Multi-layer fix:
- Layer 1: Replace WriteThrottler with TerminalWriter using requestAnimationFrame
  batching — accumulates all WebSocket data within a frame, writes once per frame
- Layer 2: DEC Mode 2026 (synchronized output) — detects ESC[?2026h/l sequences,
  buffers all data during sync blocks, flushes atomically when sync ends
- Layer 3: Write backpressure with HIGH/LOW watermarks (128KB/16KB) — sends
  pause/resume messages to server, server suppresses PTY data forwarding
- Layer 4: Viewport scroll stabilization — deferred scrollToBottom in tile scaling,
  CSS overflow-anchor: auto on xterm-viewport
- Layer 5: Existing wheel handler v7 preserved as safety net

Server changes: index.ts handles pause/resume WebSocket messages to throttle PTY output
Tests: 13 new TerminalWriter tests (RAF, sync, backpressure, dispose), 45 total passing

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
